title: 2022–23 UEFA Champions League

International football competition

The **2022–23 UEFA Champions League** was the 68th season of Europe's premier club [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") tournament organised by [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA"), and the 31st season since it was renamed from the [European Champion Clubs' Cup](/wiki/European_Champion_Clubs%27_Cup "European Champion Clubs' Cup") to the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League").

The [final](/wiki/2023_UEFA_Champions_League_final "2023 UEFA Champions League final") was played at the [Atatürk Olympic Stadium](/wiki/Atat%C3%BCrk_Olympic_Stadium "Atatürk Olympic Stadium") in [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"), Turkey, on 10 June 2023.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The stadium was originally appointed to host the [2020 UEFA Champions League final](/wiki/2020_UEFA_Champions_League_final "2020 UEFA Champions League final"), but both this and the [2021 editions](/wiki/2021_UEFA_Champions_League_final "2021 UEFA Champions League final"), which had been subsequently re-allocated to the Atatürk, were moved due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Turkey "COVID-19 pandemic in Turkey"). The 2023 final was contested by English club [Manchester City](/wiki/Manchester_City_F.C. "Manchester City F.C.") and Italian club [Inter Milan](/wiki/Inter_Milan "Inter Milan"), with the former winning 1–0 via a second-half goal by [Rodri](/wiki/Rodri_(footballer,_born_1996) "Rodri (footballer, born 1996)"), who was named [man of the match](/wiki/Player_of_the_match#Association_football "Player of the match") by UEFA. For Manchester City, this was their first-ever European Cup, and first European trophy since [1970](/wiki/1970_European_Cup_Winners%27_Cup_final "1970 European Cup Winners' Cup final"). Having earlier won the [Premier league](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_Premier_League "2022–23 Premier League") and [FA Cup](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_FA_Cup "2022–23 FA Cup") titles, they achieved a [continental treble](/wiki/Treble_(association_football)#Continental_trebles "Treble (association football)").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-BBC-5) As winners, Manchester City earned the right to play against [Sevilla](/wiki/Sevilla_FC "Sevilla FC"), the winners of the [2022–23 UEFA Europa League](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_League "2022–23 UEFA Europa League"), in the [2023 UEFA Super Cup](/wiki/2023_UEFA_Super_Cup "2023 UEFA Super Cup"), as well as qualifying for both the [2023](/wiki/2023_FIFA_Club_World_Cup "2023 FIFA Club World Cup") and [2025 FIFA Club World Cups](/wiki/2025_FIFA_Club_World_Cup "2025 FIFA Club World Cup") in Saudi Arabia and the United States, respectively.

[Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF") were the defending champions, having won a [record-extending](/wiki/European_Cup_and_UEFA_Champions_League_records_and_statistics#General_performances "European Cup and UEFA Champions League records and statistics") fourteenth European Cup title in the [previous edition](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Champions_League "2021–22 UEFA Champions League"), but they were eliminated by eventual champions Manchester City in the [semi-finals](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League_knockout_phase#Semi-finals "2022–23 UEFA Champions League knockout phase").

## Association team allocation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Association team allocation")]

A total of 78 teams from 53 of the 55 [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") member associations participated in the 2022–23 UEFA Champions League (the exceptions being Russia, who were banned from participating due to the [Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "Russian invasion of Ukraine"), and [Liechtenstein](/wiki/Liechtenstein_Football_Association "Liechtenstein Football Association"),[[Note LIE]](#cnote_Note_LIE) which did not organise a domestic league). The association ranking based on the [UEFA association coefficients](/wiki/UEFA_coefficient#Men's_association_coefficient "UEFA coefficient") was used to determine the number of participating teams for each association:[[6]](#cite_note-regulations-6)

* Associations 1–4 each had four teams qualify.
* Associations 5–6 each had three teams qualify.
* Associations 7–15 (except [Russia](/wiki/Russian_Football_Union "Russian Football Union"))[[Note RUS]](#cnote_Note_RUS) each had two teams qualify.
* Associations 16–55 (except [Liechtenstein](/wiki/Liechtenstein_Football_Association "Liechtenstein Football Association"))[[Note LIE]](#cnote_Note_LIE) each had one team qualify.
* The winners of the [2021–22 UEFA Champions League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Champions_League "2021–22 UEFA Champions League") and [2021–22 UEFA Europa League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Europa_League "2021–22 UEFA Europa League") were each given an additional entry if they did not qualify for the 2022–23 UEFA Champions League through their domestic league.

### Association ranking

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Association ranking")]

For the 2022–23 UEFA Champions League, the associations were allocated places according to their 2021 [UEFA association coefficients](/wiki/UEFA_coefficient#Men's_association_coefficient "UEFA coefficient"), which took into account their performance in European competitions from 2016–17 to 2020–21.[[7]](#cite_note-country_coefficients-7)

Apart from the allocation based on the association coefficients, associations could have additional teams participating in the Champions League, as noted below:

* (UEL) – Additional berth for UEFA Europa League title holders

Due to the suspension of Russia for the 2022–23 European season, the following changes to the access list were made:[[9]](#cite_note-rus-susp-22/23-9)

* The champions of association 11 (Scotland) enter the group stage instead of the play-off round (**Champions Path**).
* The champions of association 13 (Turkey) enter the play-off round instead of the third qualifying round (**Champions Path**).
* The champions of association 15 (Cyprus) enter the third qualifying round instead of the second qualifying round (**Champions Path**).
* The champions of associations 18 (Croatia) and 19 (Switzerland) enter the second qualifying round instead of the first qualifying round (**Champions Path**).
* The runners-up of associations 10 (Austria) and 11 (Scotland) enter the third qualifying round instead of the second qualifying round (**League Path**).

Since the [Champions League title holders](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Champions_League "2021–22 UEFA Champions League") ([Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF")) qualified via their domestic league, the following changes to the access list were made:[[9]](#cite_note-rus-susp-22/23-9)

* The champions of association 12 (Ukraine) enter the group stage instead of the play-off round (**Champions Path**).
* The champions of association 14 (Denmark) enter the play-off round instead of the third qualifying round (**Champions Path**).
* The champions of association 16 (Serbia) enter the third qualifying round instead of the second qualifying round (**Champions Path**).
* The champions of associations 20 (Greece) and 21 (Israel) enter the second qualifying round instead of the first qualifying round (**Champions Path**).

**Notes**

1. **[^](#ref_Note_LIE_1)**

   **Liechtenstein (LIE):** The seven teams affiliated with the [Liechtenstein Football Association](/wiki/Liechtenstein_Football_Association "Liechtenstein Football Association") (LFV) all played in the [Swiss football league system](/wiki/Swiss_football_league_system "Swiss football league system"). The only competition organised by the LFV was the [Liechtenstein Football Cup](/wiki/Liechtenstein_Football_Cup "Liechtenstein Football Cup") – the winners of which qualified for the [UEFA Europa Conference League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_Conference_League "UEFA Europa Conference League").
2. **[^](#ref_Note_RUS_1)**

   **Russia (RUS):** On 28 February 2022, Russian football clubs and national teams were suspended from FIFA and UEFA competitions due to the [Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "Russian invasion of Ukraine").[[10]](#cite_note-10) On 2 May 2022, UEFA confirmed that Russian clubs would be excluded from the 2022–23 UEFA competitions.[[9]](#cite_note-rus-susp-22/23-9)
3. **[^](#ref_Note_UKR_1)**

   **Ukraine (UKR):** The [2021–22 Ukrainian Premier League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Ukrainian_Premier_League "2021–22 Ukrainian Premier League") was abandoned due to the [Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "Russian invasion of Ukraine"). The top two teams of the league at the time of the abandonment ([Shakhtar Donetsk](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar_Donetsk "FC Shakhtar Donetsk") and [Dynamo Kyiv](/wiki/FC_Dynamo_Kyiv "FC Dynamo Kyiv")) were selected to play in the 2022–23 UEFA Champions League by the [Ukrainian Association of Football](/wiki/Ukrainian_Association_of_Football "Ukrainian Association of Football").

## Schedule

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Schedule")]

The schedule of the competition was as follows. All matches were played on Tuesdays and Wednesdays apart from the preliminary round final and the final. Scheduled kick-off times starting from the play-off round were 18:45 and 21:00 CEST/CET.[[11]](#cite_note-schedule-11)

As the [2022 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2022_FIFA_World_Cup "2022 FIFA World Cup") took place in Qatar between 20 November and 18 December 2022, the group stage commenced in the first week of September 2022 and concluded in the first week of November 2022 to make way for the World Cup.

The draws for the qualifying round started at 12:00 CEST/CET and were held at the UEFA headquarters in [Nyon](/wiki/Nyon "Nyon"), Switzerland.[[12]](#cite_note-12) The group stage draw took place in [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"), Turkey.[[13]](#cite_note-ExCo-13)

## Qualifying rounds

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Qualifying rounds")]

Main article: [2022–23 UEFA Champions League qualifying rounds](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round "2022–23 UEFA Champions League qualifying phase and play-off round")

### Preliminary round

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Preliminary round")]

A total of four teams played in the preliminary round. Seeding of teams was based on their 2022 [UEFA club coefficients](/wiki/UEFA_coefficient#Men's_Club_coefficient "UEFA coefficient"),[[16]](#cite_note-club_coefficients-16) with two seeded teams and two unseeded teams in the semi-final round. The matches took place at [Víkingsvöllur](/wiki/V%C3%ADkingsv%C3%B6llur "Víkingsvöllur") in [Reykjavík](/wiki/Reykjav%C3%ADk "Reykjavík"), Iceland so the first team drawn in each tie in the semi-final round, and also the final round (between the two winners of the semi-finals, whose identity was not known at the time of draw), would be the "home" team for administrative purposes. The winner of the preliminary round final advanced to the first qualifying round. The losers of the semi-finals and final were transferred to the [Europa Conference League Champions Path second qualifying round](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_Conference_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#Second_qualifying_round "2022–23 UEFA Europa Conference League qualifying phase and play-off round").

### First qualifying round

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: First qualifying round")]

The draw for the first qualifying round was held on 14 June 2022.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The first legs were played on 5 and 6 July, and the second legs were played on 12 and 13 July 2022.

The winners of the ties advanced to the Champions Path second qualifying round. The losers were transferred to the [Europa Conference League Champions Path second qualifying round](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_Conference_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#Second_qualifying_round "2022–23 UEFA Europa Conference League qualifying phase and play-off round").

### Second qualifying round

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Second qualifying round")]

The draw for the second qualifying round was held on 15 June 2022.[[18]](#cite_note-19)
The first legs were played on 19 and 20 July, and the second legs were played on 26 and 27 July 2022.

The winners of the ties advanced to the third qualifying round of their respective path. The Champions Path losers were transferred to the [Europa League Champions Path third qualifying round](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#Third_qualifying_round "2022–23 UEFA Europa League qualifying phase and play-off round"), while the League Path losers were transferred to the [Europa League Main Path third qualifying round](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#Third_qualifying_round "2022–23 UEFA Europa League qualifying phase and play-off round").

### Third qualifying round

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Third qualifying round")]

The draw for the third qualifying round was held on 18 July 2022.[[19]](#cite_note-20)
The first legs were played on 2 and 3 August, and the second legs were played on 9 August 2022.

The winners of the ties advanced to the play-off round of their respective path. The Champions Path losers were transferred to the [Europa League play-off round](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#Play-off_round "2022–23 UEFA Europa League qualifying phase and play-off round"), while the League Path losers were transferred to the [Europa League group stage](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Europa_League_group_stage "2022–23 UEFA Europa League group stage").

The draw for the group stage was held on 25 August 2022. The 32 teams were drawn into eight groups of four. For the draw, the teams were seeded into four pots, each of eight teams, based on the following principles:

* Pot 1 contained the Champions League and Europa League title holders, and the champions of the top six associations based on their [2021 UEFA country coefficients](/wiki/UEFA_coefficient#Men's_association_coefficient "UEFA coefficient"). Since the Champions League title holders, [Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF"), were also the champions of Association 2 (Spain), the champions of Association 7 (Netherlands), [Ajax](/wiki/AFC_Ajax "AFC Ajax"), were seeded into Pot 1.
* Pots 2, 3 and 4 contained the remaining teams, seeded based on their [2022 UEFA club coefficients](/wiki/UEFA_coefficient#Men's_club_coefficient "UEFA coefficient").[[16]](#cite_note-club_coefficients-16)

Teams from the same association could not be drawn into the same group.

[Eintracht Frankfurt](/wiki/Eintracht_Frankfurt "Eintracht Frankfurt") made their debut appearance in the group stage (and first appearance in the European Cup since their loss in the [1960 final](/wiki/1960_European_Cup_final "1960 European Cup final")) after winning the [2021–22 UEFA Europa League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Europa_League "2021–22 UEFA Europa League") and, as a result, this was the first time that five German clubs played in the group stage.

A total of 15 national associations were represented in the group stage. This season was the first since the [1995–96 edition](/wiki/1995%E2%80%9396_UEFA_Champions_League "1995–96 UEFA Champions League") in which a Turkish side failed to qualify for the group stage. It was also the first time since the [2007–08 season](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_UEFA_Champions_League "2007–08 UEFA Champions League") that two Scottish sides qualified for the group stage.

## Knockout phase

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Knockout phase")]

Main article: [2022–23 UEFA Champions League knockout phase](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_UEFA_Champions_League_knockout_phase "2022–23 UEFA Champions League knockout phase")

In the [knockout phase](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament"), teams played against each other over [two legs](/wiki/Two-legged_tie "Two-legged tie") on a home-and-away basis, except for the one-match final.
The mechanism of the draws for each round was as follows:

* In the draw for the round of 16, the eight group winners were seeded, and the eight group runners-up were unseeded. The seeded teams were drawn against the unseeded teams, with the seeded teams hosting the second leg. Teams from the same group or the same association cannot be drawn against each other.
* In the draws for the quarter-finals onwards, there were no seedings, and teams from the same group or the same association could be drawn against each other. As the draws for the quarter-finals and semi-finals were held together before the quarter-finals were played, the identity of the quarter-final winners was not known at the time of the semi-final draw. A draw was also held to determine which semi-final winner was designated as the "home" team for the final (for administrative purposes as it was played at a neutral venue).
